figures.caption: Table 3: Performance comparison of different model configurations across closed-domain and open-domain datasets. P: Planner, E: Executor, V: Verifier, m′: GPT-20B. For each dataset, bold indicates best performance and underline indicates second-best performance across each model.

Figure 6: Overview of the Executor Agent’s system message and description for closed-domain verification, outlining its role and guidelines for executing the verification plan and determining the final verdict.

Table 1: Water Metrics in Megalitres (ML)(1). (2) Water withdrawal is water that enters the operational water system and is used to supply the operational water demands. ... (4) Total water use is the sum of water withdrawals and water reused/recycled. Row No. column is added only for illustration purposes.
Table 4: Memory efficiency analysis for closed-domain datasets. All performance (Perf.) percentages are relative to Qw-235B w COT (0.73 on SciTab, 0.89 on SemTab).

Table 6: Macro F1 on SciTab dataset. Models marked with †, ∗, and ++ are sourced from Lu et al. (2023), Lu et al. (2024) and Wu and Feng (2024) respectively; PASTA + is from executing Gu et al. (2022a).
Figure 4: Execution trace, generated by MACE’s Executor agent for verifying the claim ‘Reused/recycled water made up 55.82 % of all operational water use across all years’ using data from Table 1.

Figure 5: Overview of the Planner Agent’s system message and description for closed-domain verification, outlining its role and guidelines for creating an action plan to verify claims from tabular data.

Figure 1: Overview of the MACE framework. Black solid arrows indicate the standard workflow sequence among agents. Black dashed arrows represent feedback loops; Red arrows show the Group Chat agent monitoring all agents. Blue dashed arrows indicate the final verdict return path from the Verifier to the User agent.

Figure 10: Overview of the Executor Agent’s system message and description for open-domain verification, outlining its role and guidelines for executing the verification plan across multiple evidence sources and determining the final verdict.
Figure 3: Action plan, generated by MACE’s Planner agent for verifying the claim ‘Reused/recycled water made up 55.82 % of all operational water use across all years’ using data from Table 1.
Table 7: Micro F1 on SemTab dataset. Models marked with † are the best performing according to Wang et al. (2021); those with ∗ are sourced from Gu et al. (2022a).
Table 9: Macro F1 on SciTab-OD dataset.

Figure 2: Snippets of the action plan generated by the Planner agent (left) and execution results from the Executor agent (right). Text in italic and colored blue are added for illustration purposes only.

Figure 11: Overview of the Verifier Agent’s system message and description for open-domain verification, outlining its role and guidelines for validating the Executor’s output and ensuring consistency before issuing the final verdict.

Figure 9: Overview of the Planner Agent’s system message and description for open-domain verification, outlining its role and guidelines for creating an action plan to verify claims from tabular data documents with retrieved evidence.

Figure 7: Overview of the Verifier Agent’s system message and description for closed-domain verification, outlining its role and guidelines for validating the Executor’s output and ensuring consistency before issuing the final verdict.
